column9: If you didn't find a suitable option above, please specify your role here ___________ (non-mandatory)

column2: Milestone Readiness Assessment by MOD Open Comments Assessment
column3:
Extract all comments listed in the procedure or milestone to prevent risks and issues for next system executions.
Prioritize open comments, minimizing risks, preventing issues, saving time.
column4:
Step 1: Collecting Data 1. Open MOD tool EF Milestone Overview Dashboard 2.0 - Spotfire and export open comments for baseline procedure or for the whole milestones in Excel format. 2. Download procedure file as PDF.

Step 2: Analysis in Copilot Chat Assess criticality of all open comments that can be blockers for smooth procedure or MS execution on the next system.

Prompt: Read the file [EPPQ.pdf]. Verify which comments from [EPPQ Comments Summary.exe] are still applicable and assess whether any of these comments represent a blocking issue for the operator to execute the procedure. Give me summary table for bocking comments with ID reference and follow up proposal. Use columns: Comment ID; Comment (Summary): Status (applicable or not), Why Blocking.

Step 3: Draft a Follow-Up Email Use Copilot in Outlook to draft a professional email summarizing the MOD feedback and outlining next steps.

Prompt: Write an email to the ME team summarizing MOD feedback and outlining next steps. Keep the tone professional and actionoriented, ready to copy paste.

column2: Milestone Readiness Assessment by MOD Containments Assessment
column3:
Verify the completeness of all containments that currently implemented or should be listed in the 
CPD procedure to prevent risks and issues during next system execution or KD13 deep dive.
Minimizing risks, preventing issues, saving time
column4:
Step 1: Collecting Data 1. Open containment dashboard EF EXE AIR dashboard - Spotfire and export data for all AIRs that have missing containments accepted ambition list for your FC. 2. Download procedure file as PDF

Step 2: Ask copilot to verify completeness and compare with AIR admin.

Prompt: Review the document / [EF Engineering - MAKE FIRST LIGHT (MFL) (Provisional).pdf - All Documents] and extract all AIR references. Compare the list of all open AIR's on this operational step from file [EF Milestone Overview Dashboard 2.0 - AIR Summary (2).csv]. Give me the table of all missing AIRs, that we should follow up and add the AIR reference to the procedure.

Step 3: Draft containment impact analysis for follow up.

Review procedure and extract all manual steps. A manual step is any action that goes beyond standard operations such as opening CPD, starting CPD, selecting scenarios, reading reports for verifying final specs, or saving MCs. Focus only on additional actions that require user intervention or manual execution outside these standard steps. Compare this manual action list.

column2: quick generate control chart (SPC) & CPK analysis with excel data
column3: During quality improvement projects, sometimes I receive shopfloor test data in excel, but take much time to apply and utilize minitab or other software to create statistical analysis. I use copilot for it and in few min it's done, the team can proceed with statistics reference and make right direction based on data talk.
column4:
Step 1 - upload excel with test data,
prompt1: I'm going to do SPC for a test, you are a six sigma master black belt, please help me to create a X bar R chart based on this excel.
prompt2:in the control chart, put spec and target value on.
prompt3:Generate capability indices (Cp, Cpk). prompt
4:give me standard diviation s 
prompt 5:draw x bar chart with x bar + -1s, and +-2s and +-3s

In my case, I stop here, of course you can continue with your analysis in following prompts, i.e. add Western Electric / Nelson rule tests, Histogram with spec overlay, Normality test & probability plot, A full SPC capability report (Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk)
column6: people who are not familiar with statistics might miss reading the information or not knowing how to use the analysis, so still people should well trained
column9: Global Quality & Excellence, Quality business partner for Tainan factory Fab

column2: Translate Quality maturity assessment model to local languages, faster and easier for shop floor engineers understanding
column3: I'm doing the quality maturity assessment in Tainan factory shop floor teams, and I found shop floor engineers asked many questions and took much time for them to read through all text and understand the meaning in the model, so I use copilot to create a dual language version and tried in one team, it went much faster and easier. And I check the quality of translation, it was really good.
column4:
Step 1 - upload the original quality maturity assessment model file (my case in ppt)

Pormpt: translate this model into a dual language model, place Chinese under English, keep the format in powerpoint, note that it's for TNF shop floor engineers. Then wait few min. 

Step2. download the dual lang version from the link copilot provide.
column6: Risk of co pilot miss understand the quality terminology (the content contains many quality specific terms), but in this case, I did not see any error translate, suggest still have a expert to do a check on dual language version..
column9: GQ&E Asia, Quality Business partner for TNF Fab
